data.narrative:
Cambridge Aerospace, a Series A defense & security frontier-tech company with $136M raised to date, is valued at approximately $1B. Valuation analysis relies on a peer set of 4 same-sector, same-stage companies (including Scale AI at $29.0B, Stark Defence at $500M, Vatn Systems at $300M, and Ares Industries at $150M), supplemented by 2 public comparables and 8 private peers in the broader aerospace and defense space.

Two triangulation methods apply. Stage-progression analysis, using total capital raised as a proxy and applying a 2.0x step-up multiplier typical of Series A advancement, implies a valuation of $272M. Peer-median analysis across the 4 same-stage, same-sector comparables yields a median valuation of $400M, though the peer range spans $150M to $29.0B, indicating significant dispersion driven by outliers like Scale AI. The peer-median approach carries elevated uncertainty due to wide variance in comparable company trajectories within defense tech.

Triangulating these two methods produces a valuation range of $231M (low) to $460M (high), with a median of $336M. The current $1B valuation significantly exceeds both methods. Key uncertainties include the representativeness of the peer set—particularly Scale AI's outlier status—and whether the stage-progression multiplier adequately reflects Cambridge Aerospace's specific technical maturity and market positioning relative to peers. The wide dispersion in defense-sector Series A valuations limits confidence in any single point estimate.
